headline: Datos Insights: Bank Wealth Units Shielded From $130B Selloff But Falling Behind Independent Channel on AI Deployment
summary: Datos Insights analyst William Trout argues that while bank wealth divisions were largely spared from the $130 billion selloff triggered by Altruist's Hazel launch — because bank advisors are captive employees, not independent practitioners choosing a custodian — the same structural inertia that protected them is now a competitive liability. The independent channel is deploying AI-native platforms (Hazel for tax planning and custodial integration, Avantos as an AI operating system for institutional wealth) while bank wealth units face three specific gaps: incomplete client data across banking silos, compliance architecture not built for AI-generated outputs, and change management for captive advisor cultures. Banks hold a structural data advantage — advisors sit alongside mortgages, credit lines, deposits, and investments — that independent RIAs cannot replicate. But that advantage is worthless until the data is connected and the AI can surface cross-sell opportunities from it.
the_so_what: Banks have a data advantage over independent RIAs — they see the full household picture (deposits, lending, trust, investments, insurance) — but Hazel and Avantos are proving that the independent channel will ship AI-native capabilities faster than banks can retrofit them. The competitive dynamic, as Datos Insights frames it, 'will not wait for bank wealth culture to catch up.'

key_stat.label: market value wiped from Raymond James, Schwab, LPL in two days after Hazel launch — bank wealth units were spared

capability_evidence.evidence: Hazel (Altruist): natively integrated AI platform combining meeting intelligence, live custodial data, and tax planning. Avantos: AI-native operating system with knowledge graph, active deployment across tens of thousands of clients at Mercer Advisors.
capability_evidence.metric: Mercer Advisors reports active Avantos deployment across tens of thousands of clients

people: William Trout — Director, Securities & Investments, Datos Insights
entities.key_topic: Bank wealth units vs independent channel AI deployment gap

_research.whats_new: Analyst perspective on WHY banks were shielded and WHY that protection is temporary. Three specific investment gaps identified.

details.claim: Three gaps: data, compliance, change management
Avantos deployed at Mercer across tens of thousands of clients
Banks see full household picture vs RIA investment-only
Competitive dynamic will not wait for bank wealth culture
Hazel built via Altruist's 2025 acquisition of Thyme
